About: It is about the abolition of state institutions and services and their replacement with private providers.
Affects: It affects the provision of legal and security services.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the tradition of the vast majority of anarchist schools of thought.
ConsistsOf: It consists of voluntary exchange and private institutions.
DevelopedBy: It was developed by Murray Rothbard.
HasA: It has concepts such as the non-aggression principle.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the abolition of state institutions.
It is characterized by the replacement of state services with private providers.
It is characterized by voluntary exchange.
It is characterized by private institutions that provide legal and security services.
It is characterized by the non-aggression principle.
It is characterized by free markets.
It is characterized by self-ownership.
It is characterized by private defense agencies and insurance companies competing to provide services.
It is characterized by a mutually agreed-upon legal code.
It is characterized by the recognition of contracts between individuals, private property, self-ownership, and tort law.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Austrian school of economics.
It was influenced by classical liberalism.
It was influenced by 19th-century American individualist anarchists.
It was influenced by mutualist thinkers such as Lysander Spooner and Benjamin Tucker.
It was influenced by Ayn Rand's anti-statist, pro–free market stances.
IsA: It is a political ideology.
It is an economic theory.
It is a voluntary society.
It is a philosophical movement.
It is a political theory.
It is a program.
It is a political, social, and economic theory.
It is a form of anarchism.
It is the only true form of anarchism.
It is a contradiction in terms.
KnownFor: It is known for advocating the abolition of state institutions and services.
It is known for advocating the replacement of state institutions and services with private providers.
It is known for its reliance on voluntary exchange and private institutions.
It is known for its emphasis on the non-aggression principle.
It is known for its support of free markets.
It is known for its advocacy of self-ownership.
It is known for its opposition to the state.
It is known for its support of the privatization of all services.
It is known for its view of capitalism and the free market as the basis for a free and prosperous society.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the United States.
PartOf: It is part of the tradition of the vast majority of anarchist schools of thought.
UsedFor: It is used for providing legal and security services.
UsedTo: It is used to abolish the State.
It is used to abolish the regularized institution of aggressive coercion.
WrittenBy: It was written by Karl Hess.
